revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:従二位]][[ko:종2위]][[zh:從二位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:従二位]][[ko:종2위]][[zh:從二位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:従二位]][[ko:종2위]][[zh:從二位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:従二位]][[ko:종2위]][[zh:從二位]]
{{Unreferenceded|date=August 2015}} '''Junior Second Rank''' (従二位, Junii) is one of the Ranks in Japan's {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|lt=hierarchy of court Ranks|ja|位階|ru|Икай|de|Hofrang_in_Japan|es|Sistema de rangos en Japón|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks in Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|qid=Q11591025|lt=divine Ranks|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It is situated below the {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Second Rank|qq=}} and above the {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == In the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} under the {{ill|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qid=Q11452077|lt=Kan'i sōtō system|1=List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles}}, Junior Second Rank, like Senior Second Rank, corresponded to the roles of {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|qid=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}} and {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|qid=Q1351174|lt=Minister of the Right|1=Minister of the Right}}. Among extralegal offices, it corresponded to positions such as {{ill|Naidaijin|en|Naidaijin|qid=Q1185338|lt=Chancellor of the Realm|1=Naidaijin}} and {{ill|Kurodo no Bettō|ja|蔵人別当|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurodo no Bettō|1=kuroudonobettou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11621871}}. Women could also be awarded this Rank, including the official wives of ministers. A notable example is {{ill|Taira no Tokiko|en|Taira no Tokiko|qid=Q3379244|lt=Taira no Tokiko|1=Taira no Tokiko}}, the principal wife of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, who was referred to as "Nun of the Second Rank." Tokiko was conferred the Rank of Junior Second Rank in recognition of the appointment of her grandson, Prince Norihito ({{ill|Emperor Takakura|en|Emperor Takakura|qid=Q357261|lt=Emperor Takakura|1=Emperor Takakura}}), as Crown Prince. At that time, Kiyomori held the position of {{ill|Gon Dainagon|ja|権大納言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon Dainagon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大納言|1=Dainagon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1354706}} and {{ill|Chūgū Daibu|ja|春宮大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūgū Daibu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春宮坊|1=Crown Prince's Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11513463}} (though he was later promoted to Chancellor).
Junior Second Rank was a high court Rank among the {{ill|nobility|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3138036}}. Among {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1975935}}, during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, it was typically granted only to the official wives of shoguns, known as {{ill|Midaidokoro|en|Midaidokoro|qid=Q841474|lt=Midaidokoro|1=Midaidokoro}}, beginning with {{ill|Hōjō Masako|en|Hōjō Masako|qid=Q463961|lt=Hōjō Masako|1=Hōjō Masako}}. Even members of the shogunal family rarely attained this Rank. Although shoguns of the {{ill|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1154406}} received this Rank, related figures such as the {{ill|Kantō kubō|en|Kantō kubō|qid=Q860777|lt=Kantō kubō|1=Kantō kubō}} were often limited to the Junior Third Rank, and even high-Ranking officials like {{ill|Kanrei|en|Kanrei|qid=Q1202497|lt=Kanrei|1=Kanrei}} typically did not rise above the Third Rank.
In the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, as the Ashikaga Shogunate's control over samurai Ranks waned, prominent daimyo increasingly lobbied directly to the imperial court, offering monetary contributions in exchange for Rank. In 1548 (Tenbun 17), {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshitaka|en|Ōuchi Yoshitaka|qid=Q1065163|lt=Ōuchi Yoshitaka|1=Ōuchi Yoshitaka}}, the most powerful guardian of western Japan, was exceptionally promoted to Junior Second Rank, despite Shogun {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|en|Ashikaga Yoshiteru|qid=Q447066|lt=Ashikaga Yoshiteru|1=Ashikaga Yoshiteru}} holding only the Junior Fourth Rank at the time.
During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|qid=Q319531|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, following {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s appointment as {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1199966}}, prominent retainers and close family members began to attain Junior Second Rank and even higher Ranks.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} onward, members of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川将軍家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳川宗家}}, including those of the {{ill|Gosanke|en|Gosanke|qid=Q1143543|lt=Three Houses of Tokugawa|1=Gosanke}} and {{ill|Gosankyō|en|Gosankyō|qid=Q1143633|lt=Three Lords of Tokugawa|1=Gosankyō}}, were occasionally promoted to Junior Second Rank or {{ill|Gon Dainagon|ja|権大納言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon Dainagon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大納言|1=Dainagon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1354706}}.
As a {{ill|posthumous court Rank|ja|栄典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/posthumous court Rank|1=Honorary distinctions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16766585}} under the {{ill|Constitution of Japan|en|Constitution of Japan|qid=Q237082|lt=Constitution of Japan|1=Constitution of Japan}}, individuals with significant contributions to the three branches of government, such as {{ill|Speaker of the House of Representatives (Japan)|en|Speaker of the House of Representatives (Japan)|qid=Q22960931|lt=Speakers of the House of Representatives|1=Speaker of the House of Representatives (Japan)}} and {{ill|President of the House of Councillors|en|President of the House of Councillors|qid=Q28869611|lt=Presidents of the House of Councillors|1=President of the House of Councillors}}, often receive this Rank posthumously. {{ill|Prime Ministers of Japan|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Ministers of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q274948}} often receive the Senior Second Rank upon death, but those with shorter tenures (generally less than one year) are often granted Junior Second Rank. In the academic field, recipients of the {{ill|Nobel Prize|en|Nobel Prize|qid=Q7191|lt=Nobel Prize|1=Nobel Prize}} and similar achievements have also been conferred this Rank.
== Notable Individuals Conferred Junior Second Rank == Dates indicate the day of Rank conferral. In cases of posthumous conferment, the individual's previous Rank is noted.
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Date of Conferment !! Notes |- | {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} || Wado 7, 5th month, 1st day (June 17, 714) || {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}. Posthumous award (Senior Third Rank). |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623185}} || Yōrō 7, 1st month, 10th day (February 19, 723) || Consort of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|qid=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}}. |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|qid=Q1890878|lt=Fujiwara no Muchimaro|1=Fujiwara no Muchimaro}} || Tenpyō 6, 1st month, 17th day (February 24, 734) || {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|qid=Q1351174|lt=Minister of the Right|1=Minister of the Right}}, promoted from Senior Third Rank. |- | {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|qid=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|1=Tachibana no Moroe}} || Tenpyō 11, 1st month, 13th day (February 25, 739) || {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|qid=Q1351174|lt=Minister of the Right|1=Minister of the Right}}, promoted from Senior Third Rank. |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Toyonari|en|Fujiwara no Toyonari|qid=Q3090678|lt=Fujiwara no Toyonari|1=Fujiwara no Toyonari}} || Tenpyō 20, 3rd month, 22nd day (April 24, 748) || {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}, promoted from Junior Third Rank. |- | {{ill|Tachibana no Konakachi|ja|橘古那可智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Konakachi|1=Tachibana no Konakachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11544216}} || Tenpyō 21, 4th month, 1st day (April 21, 749) || Consort of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|qid=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, promoted from Senior Third Rank. |}
...
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 Order of Precedence] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/e-Gov Law Search|qq=}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} Correspondence Table of Official Ranks] - {{ill|National Diet Library Modern Digital Collection|ja|国立国会図書館近代デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Modern Digital Collection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{Court Ranks of Japan}} *
{{translated page|ja|従二位|version=104429815|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:従二位]]

Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Eighth Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Senior Ninth Rank|ja|正九位|1=Senior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Ninth Rank|qq=}}
